subject: Re: Import into M, one round with options off -> 650A
body:
Some ideas for troubleshooting:

How do the half-maps look like? And the full FSC curve (not just the
resolution number)?
Have you tried varying the mask parameters?

Good luck,

--
Ricardo Diogo Righetto


Em ter., 10 de out. de 2023 às 11:09, joe dobbs <dobbsjoseph12@gmail.com>
escreveu:

> Hi Mat,
>
> I think possibly wrong pixel size during import is also counter-indicated
> by the reextraction with no problems, I don't see how it could regenerate
> properly if the coordinates were off. I'll also note that I also tried to
> manually make the star file with the same result.
>
> The mask was generated inside M from the unfiltered half-maps and seems
> reasonable to me.
>
> Thanks for the suggestions.
>
> On Tuesday, October 10, 2023 at 10:43:06 AM UTC+2 cla...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> Hi Joe,
>>
>> That sounds like it could be an incorrect pixel size. If the pixel size
>> you enter for the star file after downgrading is incorrect then it may just
>> be working with noise, although I'm not sure if that would explain why it
>> reextracts with no problems afterwards. The most recent version of Warp/M
>> now supports Relion 3.1 star files and as such you shouldn't need to use
>> the relion_star_downgrade.
>>
>> Also, does your mask look reasonable? Was it generated in M or imported
>> from something like Relion?
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Mat
>>
>>
>> On Tue, 10 Oct 2023, 09:22 joe dobbs, <dobbsj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi,
>>>
>>> I have a strange issue and wondered if any of you have seen similar:
>>>
>>> -refinement in relion of a ribosome reaches 20A
>>> -import into M (using relion_star_downgrade from dynamo2m)
>>> -one iteration with all options turned off to test the import went ok ->
>>> 650A empty looking structure (+M crashes)
>>> (as suggested in
>>> https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/ZMr-uW8bBzU/m/CweU9wrxCgAJ)
>>> -however, re-extract of the particles from M directly after import and
>>> relion_reconstruct -> ribosome is still there
>>>
>>> I don't use the pre-rotate particles option, and the star file
>>> conversion seems to have gone ok- these potential sources of error are
>>> indicated against since I can again reconstruct my structure without
>>> realignment after M export.
>>>
>>> It is then very strange that one iteration without any options affected
>>> the structure in any way- surely it should have just come back with my 20A
>>> ribosome, not some 650A thing.
>>>
>>> Has anyone seen anything like this?
>>>
>>> --
>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google
>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send
>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.

>>> .
>>>
